title: You are a Security Analyst. Review these 4 pipeline fixes for OWASP Top 10 compliance. ## CHANGES 1. G2: COEIROINK speaker map — maps VOICEVOX speaker IDs to COEIROINK UUIDs 2. G3: preprocessNarration — regex-based English→katakana conversion applied to narration text before TTS API calls 3. G4: dir...
summary_text: { "score": 2, "vulnerabilities": [ { "severity": "medium", "type": "SSRF / Untrusted URL Fetch (OWASP A10)", "description": "ImageOrchestrator trusts `generated[0]` from Freepik task polling and calls `fetch(imageUrl)` without scheme/host/IP validation. If upstream response is malicious or tampered, the runtime could be induced to fetch unintended internal/private URLs.", "remediation": "Allow only `https` URLs, enforce a strict hostname allowlist for expected Freepik/CDN domains, block loopback/private/link-local IP resolution, and enforce response size/content-type limits before download." }, { "severity": "low", "type": "Unbounded Input / Cost-DoS (OWASP A04)", "description": "`DirectionSceneSchema.image` and dialogue text are not length-bounded. `preprocessNarration()` applies many regex replacements before TTS length rejection, and image prompts are forwarded to external APIs. This is not catastrophic ReDoS, but large inputs can cause avoidable CPU/API-cost amplification.", "remediation": "Add schema max lengths (e.g., narration <= 2000 before preprocessing, image prompt <= 300-500), normalize/trim inputs early, and reject oversized payloads before regex/API calls." }, { "severity": "low", "type": "TOCTOU File Handling (OWASP A05)", "description": "Pipeline image copy flow uses `existsSync` followed by `copyFileSync`. In a local multi-process or adversarial workspace scenario, a symlink/file swap between check and copy could cause unintended file copy.", "remediation": "Avoid pre-checks; copy in `try/catch`, verify `realpath` stays under the intended directory, and `lstat` the source as a regular file immediately before copy." } ], "summary": "G2 is effectively safe (speaker ID used only as map key with safe fallback). G3 does not show practical ReDoS (regexes are static and simple), but input-size limits should be enforced earlier. G4 has limited prompt-injection impact on host integrity because data is treated as text and sanitized, but outbound prompt/data governance is still needed. G1 path traversal risk is largely mitigated by index-based filenames and workspace path controls; remaining file-handling hardening is low priority but recommended." }
